summary.brief: Booth v. Lazzara. Medical malpractice case involving emergency medicine.

evidence_index.text: On October 15, 2024, Brandi filed a negligence, wrongful death, and survivorship action against Dr. Lazzara.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dustin was later transferred to the behavioral health unit at Atrium Medical Center, where he remained until he was discharged on February 7, 2022.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Lazzara diagnosed Dustin with Bipolar Disorder I with mania and began Dustin on various medications.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Upon his discharge from the hospital, Dustin returned home where he resumed use of his vape pen and, according to Dustin's mother and Brandi, his behavior quickly deteriorated.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On February 11, 2022, police responded to a call from Brandi that Dustin was behaving strangely.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dustin engaged in a stand-off with law enforcement officers, who ultimately shot Dustin several times.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: It is my opinion, with reasonable medical certainty, that Dr. Lazzara deviated from the standard of care while treating Dustin Booth as an inpatient psychiatric patient.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Thatcher

evidence_index.text: The trial court found that, '[u]pon review of all the evidence . . . [Dr. Lazzara] acted in good faith.'
evidence_index.speaker: Trial court

evidence_index.text: Dr. Lazzara was entitled to statutory immunity pursuant to R.C. 5122.34.
evidence_index.speaker: Trial court

evidence_index.text: The trial court's grant of summary judgment in favor of Dr. Lazzara should be reversed.
evidence_index.speaker: Court of Appeals

patient_demographics.relevant_social_history: Increased use of vape pen and cannabis products

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Dustin Booth exhibited low amounts of sleep, sudden weight loss, increased motivation to complete projects, heightened interest in odd topics, and increased use of vape pen and cannabis products. Family noticed these changes in mid-January 2022.

focused_exam.mental_status: Behavioral changes noted, specifics not documented in judgment

requestables.description: Initial psychiatric evaluation by Dr. Lazzara

reveal.result_summary: Diagnosed with Bipolar Disorder I with mania
reveal.decision_impact: Led to discharge plan without addressing substance use

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient admitted to behavioral health unit with suspected mental health disorder
decision_points.prompt: What should be included in the diagnostic workup?

options.description: Include assessment for substance use disorders
options.clinical_reasoning: Substance use was a significant part of the patient's presentation

options.description: Focus solely on bipolar disorder diagnosis

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Believed mania was not attributed to substance use

expected_action_court.description: Assessment for substance use disorders should have been included
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert opinion indicated failure to diagnose substance use disorders

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failed to diagnose substance use disorders
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient ready for discharge from behavioral health unit
decision_points.prompt: What should be included in the discharge plan?

options.description: Include follow-up for substance use and community safety plan
Discharge with focus on bipolar disorder management
options.clinical_reasoning: Did not address substance use or community safety

actual_action_defendant.description: Discharged with focus on bipolar disorder management

expected_action_court.description: Discharge plan should have included substance use follow-up and safety plan
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert opinion indicated failure to ensure safety

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Discharged without addressing substance use or safety
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Comprehensive discharge plan including substance use follow-up
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient engaged in a stand-off with law enforcement and was shot

key_findings: Dr. Lazzara acted in good faith and was entitled to statutory immunity

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Thorough evaluation could have prevented adverse outcome
